name: Henergy
comment:
First 7a onsight!
On the final moves, I couldn’t spot any footholds anymore—but I definitely preferred that over baking in the sun. A few jams here and there, plus a kneebar, were definitely helpful for shaking out the pump.

Big thanks to whoever placed the quickdraws—hope it was okay to use them! If not, please don’t leave them in next time.

name: Schwesterlein Mein
comment:
The crux feels closer to 6a. It is reachy and consists of a single but demanding move that feels noticeably harder than other 5c multi-pitch routes in the area. Protection is generally solid, though some sections contain loose rock, so care is advised.

The highlight is definitely the final pitch, featuring a few delicate moves on small crimps, precise footwork, and a short micro layback. very enjoyable climbing.

If you have double ropes and no parties below, abseiling is recommended. The descent via the Gasserl is feasible but somewhat tedious and time-consuming.
